heading: Sampling and averaging; determination of weighted average dumping margin and countervailable subsidy rate
label: 1677f–1 Sampling and averaging; determination of weighted average dumping margin and countervailable subsidy rate

children.text: use averaging and statistically valid samples, if there is a significant volume of sales of the subject merchandise or a significant number or types of products, and
decline to take into account adjustments which are insignificant in relation to the price or value of the merchandise.

children.heading: Selection of averages and samples
children.label: b Selection of averages and samples
children.text: The authority to select averages and statistically valid samples shall rest exclusively with the administering authority. The administering authority shall, to the greatest extent possible, consult with the exporters and producers regarding the method to be used to select exporters, producers, or types of products under this section.

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: In determining weighted average dumping margins under section 1673b(d), 1673d(c), or 1675(a) of this title, the administering authority shall determine the individual weighted average dumping margin for each known exporter and producer of the subject merchandise.

children.label: A
children.text: a sample of exporters, producers, or types of products that is statistically valid based on the information available to the administering authority at the time of selection, or

children.label: B
children.text: exporters and producers accounting for the largest volume of the subject merchandise from the exporting country that can be reasonably examined.

children.heading: Determination of less than fair value
children.label: d Determination of less than fair value
i
children.text: by comparing the weighted average of the normal values to the weighted average of the export prices (and constructed export prices) for comparable merchandise, or

children.label: ii
children.text: by comparing the normal values of individual transactions to the export prices (or constructed export prices) of individual transactions for comparable merchandise.

children.label: i
children.text: there is a pattern of export prices (or constructed export prices) for comparable merchandise that differ significantly among purchasers, regions, or periods of time, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the administering authority explains why such differences cannot be taken into account using a method described in paragraph (1)(A)(i) or (ii).

children.heading: Reviews
children.label: 2 Reviews
children.text: In a review under , when comparing export prices (or constructed export prices) of individual transactions to the weighted average price of sales of the foreign like product, the administering authority shall limit its averaging of prices to a period not exceeding the calendar month that corresponds most closely to the calendar month of the individual export sale.section 1675 of this title

children.heading: Determination of countervailable subsidy rate
children.label: e Determination of countervailable subsidy rate

children.heading: General rule
children.label: 1 General rule
children.text: In determining countervailable subsidy rates under section 1671b(d), 1671d(c), or 1675(a) of this title, the administering authority shall determine an individual countervailable subsidy rate for each known exporter or producer of the subject merchandise.

children.label: i
children.text: a sample of exporters or producers that the administering authority determines is statistically valid based on the information available to the administering authority at the time of selection, or

children.label: ii
children.text: exporters and producers accounting for the largest volume of the subject merchandise from the exporting country that the administering authority determines can be reasonably examined; or

children.label: B
children.text: determine a single country-wide subsidy rate to be applied to all exporters and producers.

children.heading: Adjustment of antidumping duty in certain proceedings relating to imports from nonmarket economy countries
children.label: f Adjustment of antidumping duty in certain proceedings relating to imports from nonmarket economy countries
A
children.text: pursuant to , a countervailable subsidy (other than an export subsidy referred to in ) has been provided with respect to the class or kind of merchandise,section 1671(a)(1) of this titlesection 1677a(c)(1)(C) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: such countervailable subsidy has been demonstrated to have reduced the average price of imports of the class or kind of merchandise during the relevant period, and

children.label: C
children.text: the administering authority can reasonably estimate the extent to which the countervailable subsidy referred to in subparagraph (B), in combination with the use of normal value determined pursuant to , has increased the weighted average dumping margin for the class or kind of merchandise,section 1677b(c) of this title

children.heading: Maximum reduction in antidumping duty
children.label: 2 Maximum reduction in antidumping duty
children.text: The administering authority may not reduce the antidumping duty applicable to a class or kind of merchandise from a nonmarket economy country under this subsection by more than the portion of the countervailing duty rate attributable to a countervailable subsidy that is provided with respect to the class or kind of merchandise and that meets the conditions described in subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of paragraph (1).
